home.hero.h1:
You've built a real business.
Now what?
home.hero.sub: Grow it, streamline it, or finally step back from it. Whatever the move is, I can help you get there.
home.how.heading:
Your operations
and technology
partner.
home.how.lede: {n} years hands-on across the startup ecosystem — designing customer experiences, shaping product strategy, and shipping software. I bring all of it to your business.
home.whatido.heading:
What most operators
need three people for.
home.whatido.lede: Strategy and execution. Design and development. The full picture — without the headcount.
home.whatido.cx.title: Customer Experience
home.whatido.cx.body: Your customers have options. If finding you, booking with you, or paying you feels like friction — they'll find someone easier. Let's make sure every touchpoint works the way it should.
home.whatido.ops.title: Operations & Process
home.whatido.ops.body: You're spending time on things that shouldn't need you. Approvals, follow-ups, manual steps that made sense when you were smaller — they're costing you now. Let's find them and fix them.
home.whatido.tech.title: Technology
home.whatido.tech.body: You've probably got tools that don't talk to each other, or a gap you're filling with a spreadsheet. Let's sort out what you actually need, set it up properly, and make sure your team actually uses it.
home.whatido.growth.title: Growth
home.whatido.growth.body: You know there's more on the table — you just haven't had the bandwidth to go after it. Pricing, retention, new markets. Let's find the levers worth pulling and go pull them.
home.work.heading: Selected work.
home.work.card.title: A cash business that now runs without its owner in the room.
home.work.card.problem: The problem: A profitable services business that only worked when the owner was physically present — service slipped, revenue softened, and theft was a constant tax.
home.work.card.work: The work: 2+ years embedded as product consultant. Designed and shipped a custom operating system end-to-end — order management, finance, scheduling, payroll, loyalty — then led the rebuild onto custom code once the playbook was proven.
footer.cta.heading:
Your business
runs. Let's make
it scale.
footer.tagline:
Operator's growth partner.
Based in Montreal, working across Canada and Latin America.
about.hero.h1:
I help operators
get the growth layer
their business deserves.
about.hero.p1: I'm Michael. I've spent {n} years hands-on in tech — designing customer experiences, building product strategy, and shipping software inside high-growth startups across North America.
about.hero.p2: Now I work independently with owner-operators who are profitable and ready to grow, but don't have the in-house capability to make it happen.
about.hero.p3: My engagements range from scoped consulting to retained partnerships to equity arrangements — depending on the fit. I only work with two or three clients at a time, so fit matters: I want to be deep in the work, not spread thin across it. The businesses I'm drawn to have real customers, real cash flow, and meaningful upside still on the table — usually through better operations, smarter systems, and growth strategies they just haven't had the right partner to pursue yet.
about.hero.p4: I work in English and French — comfortable running engagements, presentations, and discovery sessions in either.
about.hero.p5: Outside of work: I'm obsessed with my garden, feeding the bees, and strolling in nature 🌱
about.career.heading: Where I've been.
about.career.lede: Five companies. Just over $200M in venture financing raised by the products and teams I helped build. Now putting that experience to work for owner-operators.
about.career.hb.note: Led full platform redesign and rebuild; drove transformation to empowered product model.
about.career.amori.note: Co-founded; advised on tech stack, GTM, analytics.
about.career.cox.note: ~99% reduction in theft. 266% YoY gross profit growth. End-to-end custom operating system.
about.career.rr.note: Seed → $142M Series B. Led design system, ecom build, supply/pricing product strategy.
about.career.nurx.note: Seed (Y Combinator) → $36M Series B. Designed full app, operations console, scaled from 1 to 14+ states. Acquired by Thirty Madison in 2022 — which was acquired by Remedy Meds in 2025.
about.close.heading:
Real customers.
Real cash flow.
Real upside.
about.close.lede: If that sounds like your business — and you're ready to grow without the overhead of a full in-house team — let's talk.
services.phil.heading:
Outcome-first.
Not delivery-first.
services.phil.row.scope.left: Scope a deliverable — website, app, strategy doc
services.phil.row.scope.right: Scope an outcome — more customers, lower churn, faster ops
services.phil.row.deliver.left: Deliver the thing, invoice, move on
services.phil.row.deliver.right: Stay until the outcome is real — measure, adjust, iterate
services.phil.row.maintain.right: You own the result — the tool is a means, not the point
services.phil.row.fixed.left: Fixed scope, regardless of whether it works
services.phil.row.fixed.right: We choose the best path together, not the most billable one
services.phil.quote: "I won't take your money to build a website, platform, or app if I don't believe it will deliver the outcome you're after. Tech is a lever. The outcome is the point."
services.engage.heading:
Three ways to
work together.
services.engage.lede: We pick whichever shape fits the work. Most engagements start small and grow. I only work with two or three clients at a time — so fit matters before format.
services.engage.consulting.label: Consulting
services.engage.consulting.head: Scoped and clean.
services.engage.consulting.body: Day or week rates. Best for a specific problem or something specific to ship. Low commitment, fast clarity. A great starting point that often leads to deeper work.
services.engage.retainer.label: Retainer + % of impact
services.engage.retainer.head: Skin in the game.
services.engage.retainer.body: I embed with your team. My upside is tied to the results we create together. Medium-term, fully accountable.
services.engage.equity.label: Retainer + equity
services.engage.equity.head: Full partnership.
services.engage.equity.body: Long-horizon alignment. For operators ready to go deep and build something together — where I'm sharing in the upside alongside you.
services.cap.heading:
Four capabilities,
one operator.
services.cap.cx.title: Customer Experience Design
services.cap.cx.body: How your customers find you, book with you, pay you, and come back. I redesign the experience end-to-end so it works for you and feels right for them.
services.cap.ops.title: Operations & Process
services.cap.ops.body: Where is the friction? Where are you paying for something that could run itself? I map it, redesign it, and help automate what is costing you time and money.
services.cap.tech.title: Software & Tech Implementation
services.cap.tech.body: The right tools, connected properly, actually adopted by the team. Selection, configuration, implementation — no bloat, no platform you regret in 18 months.
services.cap.growth.title: Growth & Expansion Strategy
services.cap.growth.body: Pricing, retention, new markets, new revenue lines. Data-backed decisions, not gut-feel. We measure what is working, double down, and cut what is not.
services.fit.heading:
Honest before
the call.
services.fit.good.owner: You own or operate the business
services.fit.good.revenue: Revenue is real, and so are the problems
services.fit.good.outgrown: You've outgrown the tools and processes you started with
services.fit.good.unsure: You know something's being left on the table, but don't know where to start
services.fit.not.saas: Pure SaaS startups (not my focus right now)
services.fit.not.execute: Looking for someone to just build or execute
services.close.heading:
Sound like
a fit?
services.close.lede: 30 minutes, no pitch. You tell me what's going on in the business. I tell you if I can help.
book.hero.lede: You tell me what's going on in the business. I tell you if I think I can help. If I can't, I'll say so — and where possible, point you to someone who can.
book.local.heading: Based in Montréal or the Laurentides?
book.local.body: For owner-operators in the area, I'm happy to do the discovery session in person — at your office, your shop, or wherever the work actually happens. Walking the floor tells me more in 30 minutes than a video call does in an hour. Mention it when you book and we'll set it up.
book.cal.tz: All times shown in your local timezone
cs.hero.h1:
A cash business that
now runs without
its owner in the room.
cs.meta.client.label: Client
cs.meta.engagement.label: Engagement
cs.meta.approach.label: Approach
cs.meta.cap.label: Capabilities
cs.stat.profit.label: YoY gross profit growth
cs.stat.theft.label: Reduction in theft
cs.stat.mvp.label: To first MVP
cs.stat.away.label: Owner can be away
cs.start.heading:
A healthy
business with
a fragile
backbone.
cs.start.p1: The business was profitable. The owner was experienced. But the operation only worked when he was physically present.
cs.start.p2: It's a cash-based services business — the kind that has been around for decades, with real customers and steady demand. Strong years had happened. But every time he stepped away for travel, even for a few weeks, things slowly came apart. Service quality slipped. Revenue softened. He'd come home and spend weeks pulling it back together.
cs.start.sympt.away: Whenever the owner left town, service degraded
cs.start.sympt.theft: Theft was a constant tax on the business
cs.start.sympt.patchwork: A patchwork of paper, spreadsheets, and unfinished software
cs.start.sympt.nofit: No buy-it-now product on the market fit how the business actually ran
cs.why.heading:
Why we chose to build,
not buy.
cs.why.buy.label: The buy path
cs.why.buy.lead: We surveyed the market. Every off-the-shelf tool we found either solved 30–50% of the problem, or it asked the business to bend around the software.
cs.why.buy.body: Because of the specifics of how this operation runs, no SaaS product could scale to the long-term need without major compromise. The cost of bending the business to fit the tool would have been higher than the cost of building.
cs.why.build.label: The build path
cs.why.build.lead: Build something custom on a sophisticated app-builder stack — fast enough to ship in weeks, flexible enough to evolve for years. (This was pre-AI — no shortcuts; just deliberate design and disciplined iteration)
cs.why.build.body: The app-builder layer let us avoid the cost of a full custom engineering team while keeping the freedom to design exactly what the business needed. The owner kept ownership. We kept the iteration speed.
cs.timeline.heading:
One month to MVP.
Two years of compounding.
cs.timeline.mvp.title: Discovery, planning, MVP
cs.timeline.mvp.body: Mapped the business end-to-end and identified the biggest levers, plus the foundational pieces that had to be right for the next two years — particularly data design and integrity. We started where the lifeblood was: order tracking and the movement of money — by who, when, and for what. The first MVP shipped in one month.
cs.timeline.leak.title: Stop the leak
cs.timeline.leak.body: Closed the gaps theft was hiding in — every transaction, adjustment, and shift now visible and reconciled. For the first time, the business had reliable cash-flow visibility without guesswork or hours per day on paper and spreadsheets.
cs.timeline.system.title: Run the business from the system
cs.timeline.system.body: Order management, payroll, customer loyalty, finance history, and scheduling — moved off of side tools and into one place. Layered in service ratings and a rewards program for top performers, which lifted both retention and the level of service the team delivered.
cs.timeline.custom.title: Graduating to custom code
cs.timeline.custom.body: After two years on the app-builder stack, we had validated every business case, workflow, and automation. We always knew the long game would be a custom-coded core for scalability cases the app builder couldn't cover — we just wanted certainty before taking that bet. With the playbook proven, I designed and led a developer team to rebuild the core platform on custom code. The business runs on it today, and is still thriving.
cs.timeline.custom.out: Custom platform live · still in use
cs.cap.heading:
Seven surfaces.
One operating layer.
cs.cap.lede: What started as a single MVP now runs nearly every part of the business.
cs.cap.order.title: Order management
cs.cap.order.body: Every order, every adjustment, every status — captured and visible.
cs.cap.payroll.title: Payroll
cs.cap.payroll.body: Hours, rates, and pay flowing through the same system that runs operations.
cs.cap.loyalty.title: Customer loyalty
cs.cap.loyalty.body: Repeat-customer program tuned over time, built into the daily flow.
cs.cap.finance.title: Finance history
cs.cap.finance.body: Years of clean transactional history — no more reconstructing from receipts.
cs.cap.analytics.title: Analytics dashboards
cs.cap.analytics.body: The owner sees what is happening today, this week, this quarter — without asking anyone.
cs.cap.sched.title: Smart scheduling
cs.cap.sched.body: Built to reduce no-shows and optimize utilization — fewer empty slots, fewer last-minute scrambles.
cs.cap.staff.title: Staff performance
cs.cap.staff.body: Service ratings, rewards for top performers, and clear baselines that surface who needs coaching.
cs.outcome.heading:
The business runs
whether he's there
or not.
cs.outcome.p1: One year in, gross profit had grown 266% — not revenue, profit. Theft had effectively disappeared because every cash movement was reconciled in the system. With service ratings, rewards for top performers, and clear coaching baselines, retention climbed and the level of service rose with it.
cs.outcome.p2: Two years in, the owner can leave the business for months without anything changing. The system holds. The team holds. Revenue holds. He travels, returns, and the business is exactly where he left it — sometimes better.
cs.outcome.quote: "I don't deliver software. I deliver businesses that don't fall apart when the owner walks out of the room."
cs.close.heading:
If your business
only works when
you're in the room —
let's talk.
cs.close.lede: 30 minutes, no pitch. You tell me what's going on. I tell you if I think I can help.
